title: coreopsis hamiltonii
intro: Coreopsis hamiltonii, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Coreopsis hamiltonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
